inputText: I lead comms for a nonprofit focused on migration between the US and Colombia. I read The New York Times, Reuters, and El Tiempo every morning. I also follow @StateDept on X for official updates.
I handle communications for an energy association tracking sanctions in Venezuela and Colombia. I monitor Reuters, Bloomberg, and Financial Times, and I follow @USTreasury and @CancilleriaCol on X.
I brief executives on security cooperation in Mexico and the US. My core sources are Washington Post, Wall Street Journal, and AP News. On social, I track @DHSgov and @CBP.
I run public affairs for a health NGO working in Brazil and Colombia. I read Folha de S.Paulo, El País, and BBC News. I also follow @WHO and @MinSaludCol on social.
I support a trade group focused on customs policy across Peru, Chile, and Colombia. I rely on Reuters, Diario Financiero, and Portafolio. I follow @SUNATOficial and @DIANColombia for updates.
I cover migration headlines tied to deportation flights and asylum court changes. I usually read Reuters and Semana, and I keep an eye on @ICEgov.
My updates focus on sanctions and compliance risk for clients in the region. I start with Financial Times and Bloomberg, then check @USTreasury for official notices.
I follow bilateral storylines and diplomatic tone shifts between capitals. My baseline is El País and AP, plus @StateDept and @CancilleriaCol.
I monitor security incidents near border corridors and official reactions. I use Washington Post, Reuters, and @CBP.
I track public health messaging during outbreaks and vaccine updates. I read WHO bulletins, BBC, and follow @WHO.

I run comms for a manufacturing coalition and I'm juggling way too many threads right now: labor disputes, customs delays, and political noise around new tariffs. Most of my briefs are about Colombia, Mexico, and the US. I read Reuters, WSJ, and Portafolio; when things move fast I also check @USTRadeRep and @DIANColombia.

For context: I advise an NGO board on migration and humanitarian operations. The same week I'm tracking shelter capacity, deportation notices, and visa announcements touching Colombia, Panama, and Ecuador. I read El Tiempo, AP, and BBC; for social feeds I mainly watch @UNHCR and @MigracionCol.

I handle strategic comms in energy. My team keeps mixing up what matters, so I track sanctions, compliance, and shipping risk tied to Venezuela and Colombia. Daily reads: Bloomberg, Financial Times, Reuters. Social accounts: @USTreasury, @PDVSA, and @MinEnergiaCo.

I'm less interested in broad headlines and more in narrative drift on security cooperation. For this quarter I'm focused on US-Colombia-Mexico coordination, especially around border pressure and organized crime framing. My sources are Washington Post, El País, and Reuters; on X I monitor @DHSgov and @PoliciaColombia.

My workflow is messy: morning clips, noon stakeholder calls, late-night edits. But what I really monitor is public health policy and vaccine messaging in Brazil, Colombia, and Peru. I rely on Folha de S.Paulo, Semana, and BBC; plus @WHO and @MinSaludCol when decisions break.
I need broad awareness and usually just skim headlines when I can.
I follow the White House on X and read Reuters.
Mostly tracking sanctions and migration in US and Colombia. Sources: NYT, New York Times, Reuters, @StateDept, @statedept.
I monitor 'El Tiempo' and @ELTIEMPO for updates on Colombia and Peru elections.
Reading AP + Associated Press + AP News, and checking @WHO + @who. Focus: vaccine rollout in Brazil.
